summary: Russia launched a record attack on Kyiv using 28 ballistic missiles, resulting in 11 confirmed deaths and over 30 injuries. Ukrainian forces targeted the Kstovsky refinery, which produces approximately 11% of Russia's gasoline. Russian forces report the liberation of Piskunovka in the DPR and the settlement of Ukrainske in the Vovchansk district.
key_points: Russia launched a record 28 ballistic missiles at Kyiv in a single attack, causing 11 deaths and over 30 injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82326, @eRadarrua/99359).
The Kstovsky refinery, producing 11% of Russia's gasoline and one-third of Moscow's fuel needs, was hit (Source: @UkraineNow/82332).
Russian forces report the liberation of Piskunovka in the DPR and the settlement of Ukrainske in the Vovchansk district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31327, @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Jet-powered UAVs targeted Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, and Mykolaiv, with some originating from the Bryansk region (Source: @kpszsu/67079, @eRadarrua/99327, @kpszsu/67105).
Russian forces report small-arms battles in Bachevsk, Pisarevka, Marino, Novaya Sech, and Khoten in the Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Ukrainian UAV strikes in Rylsk, Kursk region, killed one OMON technician and wounded six people (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Over 52,500 people, including 4,500 children, sought shelter in the Kyiv metro during the recent Russian attack (Source: @eRadarrua/99362).
The Russian Defense Ministry claims a strike on a Kyiv factory producing control systems for 'Flamingo' and 'Fire Point' missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31321).

sections.executive_summary: Russia conducted a record-breaking strike on Kyiv using 28 ballistic missiles, causing significant civilian casualties and damaging over 20 residential buildings. Ukraine retaliated by striking the Kstovsky refinery, a critical fuel hub providing one-third of Moscow's gasoline. Russian forces report territorial gains in the DPR and the Vovchansk district while continuing small-arms battles in the Sumy region.

points: Kyiv suffered a massive strike resulting in 11 confirmed deaths and over 30 injuries, with over 20 residential buildings damaged and a nine-story building partially destroyed in the Darnitsky district (Source: @UkraineNow/82326).

The National Bank of Ukraine is seeking the dismissal of Ukrposhta CEO Smilyanskyi, considering the suspension of the company's payment license if the Ministry of Community Development ignores the demand (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31299).
Ombudsman Lubinets reported that 93% of inspected shelters have defects, with some shelters designed for 500 people only accommodating 20 (Source: @UkraineNow/82320).
A sociological poll by 'Rating' Group in June 2026 indicates 77% of Ukrainians disapprove of TCC activities, with 53% stating they completely disapprove (Source: @UkraineNow/82324).
Ombudsman Lubinets called for the urgent return of 46 severely wounded Ukrainian prisoners of war who were injured during the July 2022 Olenivka colony attack (Source: @UkraineNow/82322).
The Kstovsky refinery, the fourth largest in Russia producing 11% of the country's gasoline, was hit, potentially deepening the fuel crisis in the Moscow region (Source: @UkraineNow/82332).
In Rylsk, Kursk region, an OMON explosives technician was killed and a police officer wounded while attempting to defuse a warhead dropped by Ukrainian UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Russian air defenses shot down 7 UAVs in the Leningrad region, 5 on approach to Moscow, and over 25 in the Rostov region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
The Russian Defense Ministry claims to have struck a factory in Kyiv that produces control systems for 'Flamingo' and 'Fire Point' missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31321).
The Russian Ministry of Health plans to expand neonatal screening to include five additional diseases to identify hereditary and congenital conditions (Source: @tass_agency/383362).
The Russian army reports the liberation of Piskunovka in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31327).
In the Vovchansk sector, fighting continues in Zemlyanom Yar and the cemetery area of Losevka village (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
The settlement of Ukrainske in the Vovchansk district has been reported as liberated by Russian forces (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Russian assault units continue offensive operations in Kazachaya Lopan and adjacent forest areas on the Kharkiv direction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Small-arms battles are ongoing in the Velyky Burluk sector, specifically in Petro-Ivanovka and forest areas near Budarky and Zemlyanky (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Russian 'North' group forces are engaged in small-arms battles in Bachevsk and surroundings in the Shostka district of the Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Fighting is reported in Pisarevka, Marino, the village of Novaya Sech, and the settlement of Khoten in the Sumy district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Small-arms battles continue in forest areas of the Krasnopollye district in the Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Sumy region residents report widespread electricity and water outages in apartment buildings since June 30 following intensified Russian strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Jet-powered UAVs were tracked moving from the Bryansk region toward the Chernihiv region and Kyiv (Source: @eRadarrua/99327, @eRadarrua/99315).
UAVs were tracked moving toward Zaporizhzhia from the south (Source: @kpszsu/67079).
UAVs were tracked moving toward Mykolaiv from the south and toward the Odesa region from the Permomaiskyi district of Mykolaiv region (Source: @kpszsu/67105, @kpszsu/67088).
Power supply issues were reported in Crimea, with blackouts in Feodosia and Armyansk and repair work ongoing in Saki (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Russia launched a record 28 ballistic missiles at Kyiv, resulting in 11 deaths and over 30 injuries (Source: @eRadarrua/99359, @UkraineNow/82326).

Jet-powered 'Shahed' UAVs were detected over Kyiv, moving through areas including Akademmistechko, Shulyavka, Syrets, Kurenivka, and the Lisovyi massif (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68491, @mon1tor_ua/68462, @mon1tor_ua/68488).
Jet-powered UAVs were tracked passing Slavutych toward the Kyiv reservoir and moving from Nizhyn toward Kulikivka (Source: @eRadarrua/99291, @eRadarrua/99325).
KABs (guided aerial bombs) were launched toward the south-eastern direction of the Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/67104).
Vodafone reported potential fixed internet disruptions, including home internet and contact center issues, following the Russian night attack (Source: @UkraineNow/82330).

key_developments: Russia launched a record 28 ballistic missiles at Kyiv, killing 11 and injuring over 30, damaging 20+ residential buildings (Source: @eRadarrua/99359, @UkraineNow/82326).
Ukraine struck the Kstovsky refinery, which produces 11% of Russia's gasoline and provides one-third of Moscow's fuel needs (Source: @UkraineNow/82332).
Russian forces report the liberation of Piskunovka in the DPR and the settlement of Ukrainske in the Vovchansk district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31327, @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Jet-powered UAVs were deployed in a massive strike targeting Kyiv, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv, and Pavlograd (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Russian 'North' group forces are conducting small-arms battles in Bachevsk, Pisarevka, Marino, Novaya Sech, and Khoten in the Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Ukrainian UAV strikes in Rylsk, Kursk region, killed one OMON explosives technician and wounded six people (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31315).
Russian Defense Ministry claims to have destroyed a Kyiv factory producing control systems for 'Flamingo' and 'Fire Point' missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31321).
sections.threat_assessment: The use of a record number of ballistic missiles and jet-powered UAVs indicates a Russian effort to overwhelm Kyiv's air defenses. The targeting of energy and industrial infrastructure, such as the claimed missile control factory, suggests a strategy of attrition. The intensification of small-arms battles in the Sumy region indicates active Russian pressure on the northern border.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is focusing on strategic strikes against Russian energy infrastructure, as seen with the Kstovsky refinery hit to disrupt Moscow's fuel supply. Internally, the Ukrainian government faces criticism regarding the quality of shelters and TCC activities. Internationally, Germany's Boris Pistorius has stated the conflict has entered a 'decisive phase' and Ukraine requires more funding (Source: @tass_agency/383349).
sections.intelligence_notes: The deployment of jet-powered UAVs is specifically noted as complicating air defense operations. The high volume of civilians (52,500) seeking shelter in the Kyiv metro underscores the perceived severity of the threat. The reported liberation of Ukrainske and Piskunovka suggests incremental Russian territorial gains in the East.
